title: Location Areas, Encounter Relations, Filtering, and Exploration · Independent Go Trial
summary: An independent conservation group receives a different region, command sequence, API fixture, cache age, concurrency budget, accessibility need, platform, and injected failure and transfers location areas, encounter relations, filtering, and exploration into a changed provenance-preserving location encounter explorer. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of provenance-preserving location encounter explorer with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress throu…
objectives: Distinguish region, location, and location-area resources and map the command to the endpoint whose relation answers the user need.

Deduplicate Pokemon by stable identity while retaining area, version, method, chance, or condition provenance needed by the view.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Pokedex Go Location Resource M…
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go team handles case 7770 during a fair-use API stewardship review. Build deterministic Go evidence to admit bounded location-area encounters and preserve relation provenance through display; browser code must remain deterministic and side-effect-free; then inject one redirect, control sequence, file, timeout, or persistence failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
